summary:
The query about REGN5458 being a single-targeted antibody cannot be supported due to lack of documentation; the compound may be confused with REGN-EB3, a combination therapy involving three distinct monoclonal antibodies. No credible evidence confirms the existence or single-targeting nature of REGN5458. 

Note: This response reflects an assessment grounded solely in available data—no external assumptions are made beyond what can be derived from retrieval results.

There is no record of REGN5458 existing within the dataset — references pertain instead to REGN-EB3, comprising at least three separate monoclonal antibodies against different epitopes of Ebola GP protein. Consequently, since neither the identity nor targeted action of REGN5458 has been established through verifiable sources, asserting it behaves as a single-targeted therapeutic remains unsupported by clinical evidence.
